info.title: Configuration operator Iopole API
info.description:
## Iopole API

To use the Iopole APIs, you must have a **`clientId`** and a **`clientSecret`**.

You can obtain these credentials by one of the following methods:
- 📧 Contact the Iopole team at **support@iopole.com**
- 🧪 Connect to **[Iopole Labs](https://labs.iopole.io)** to create your own sandbox environment

### 📚 API Documentation
- 🧪 **Preproduction**: [https://docs.ppd.iopole.fr/](https://docs.ppd.iopole.fr/)
- 🚀 **Production**: [https://docs.iopole.com/](https://docs.iopole.com/)

### 🛠️ Support & Monitoring (production only)
- 📈 **System status**: [https://uptime.iopole.com](https://uptime.iopole.com)
  - View real-time platform availability
  - Subscribe to incident notifications
  - Receive alerts for planned maintenance periods
- 📝 **Release notes**: [https://release.iopole.com](https://release.iopole.com)

get.summary: Retrieve list of operator business entity
get.description:
🎯 **Purpose**

Retrieve the list of **business entities owned and managed by the current operator**.

🏢 **Operator business entities directory**

This endpoint represents the operator’s **internal directory**.
Use this endpoint to **list and manage the business entities** associated with the authenticated operator.

parameters.name: customer-id
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
🔎 **Search query**

Define the **search expression** used to filter the operator’s business entities.
The query syntax and supported operators are described here:
- 📘 [Search usage](https://docs.ppd.iopole.fr/docs/iopole-api/search)

> ✍️ **Examples**
> 
> - Search by entity identifier: `identifierValue:"123456789"`
> - Search by identifier and country: `identifierValue:"123456789" AND country:"FR"`
> - Search by name with wildcard and entity type: `name:"HEL*" AND type:"LEGAL_UNIT"`

schema.description: {"mdLink":"[Refer to page filter](../../search)","childCloseName":"Hide available field","childOpenName":"Open available field","child":[{"label":"name","type":"string","text":"Corporate name of the company. Search is case insensitive. Supports the wildcard character '*'. A minimum of 3 characters is required.","valueDescription":"Minimum 3 characters (without wildcard)"},{"label":"identifierScheme","text":"The business entity identifier scheme.","type":"string","valueDescription":"Must be a 4 digit string"},{"label":"identifierValue","text":"Legal identifier of the company","type":"string"},{"label":"country","type":"string (2 char)","text":"Country ISO code","link":{"placeholder":"Country iso code list","link":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2#Officially_assigned_code_elements"}},{"label":"type","type":"enum","text":"Company's type","array":[{"label":"Represents a distinct legal entity, such as a legally registered entity with a unique identifier.","code":"LEGAL_UNIT"},{"label":"Refers to a specific physical or operational location within a legal unit, such as a branch, department, or regional office.","code":"OFFICE"}]}]}

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description:
⏭️ **Offset** : Starting position in the result set

**Default / Min / Max:** 0 / 0 / 1000
⚠️Large offset values may impact performance

**Example**
`offset=500&limit=100` → skips 500 records and returns the next 100.

schema.description:
⏭️ **Offset** : Starting position in the result set

**Default / Min / Max:** 0 / 0 / 1000
⚠️Large offset values may impact performance

**Example**
`offset=500&limit=100` → skips 500 records and returns the next 100.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
🔢 **Limit** : Maximum number of records returned

**Default / Min / Max:** 50 / 1 / 200

**Example:** `offset=0&limit=50`
Returns the first 50 records.

schema.description:

get.summary: Retrieve an operator business entity for a given id
get.description:
🎯 **Purpose**

Retrieve the details of a **single business entity** identified by `businessEntityId`,
owned and managed by the **current authenticated operator**.

🏢 **Operator business entity directory**

This endpoint provides access to the operator’s **internal directory** for a specific business entity.
Use this endpoint to **consult and manage the data** associated with the given business entity.

parameters.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

parameters.description: The business entity id.

delete.description:
🗑️️ **Removal conditions**

A business entity can be removed **only if ALL of the following conditions are met**:

- The entity is **not claimed by any operator**, or is **claimed only by the current operator**.
- There are **no active office entities** linked to the legal unit.
- The entity is **not a French entity** (managed by the government directory synchronisation).
- The entity has **no active network registrations**.

If any of these conditions is not satisfied, the removal request will be **rejected**.

post.description:
🎯 **Purpose**

Create a **new legal unit** in the operator directory (no claim required).

⚠️**🇫🇷French reform context**

This endpoint **must NOT be used** in the context of the **French e-invoicing reform**.
To onboard or associate a legal unit in this context, use the **claim endpoint** instead.

⚠️ **Important – Enrollment vs registration**

This endpoint **must NOT be used** if the business entity has already been enrolled
through the **enrollment endpoint**.
Enrollment automatically handles all creation.

post.description:
🎯 **Purpose**

Create a **office** in the operator directory (no claim required).

⚠️**🇫🇷️️French reform context**

This endpoint **must NOT be used** in the context of the **French e-invoicing reform**.
To onboard or associate a legal unit in this context, use the **claim endpoint** instead.

⚠️ **Important – Enrollment vs registration**

This endpoint **must NOT be used** if the business entity has already been enrolled
through the **enrollment endpoint**.
Enrollment automatically handles all creation.

post.summary: Configure an operator business entity management
post.description:
🎯 **Purpose**

Use this endpoint to **claim (associate) a French or international business entity**
with the current operator.
If the business entity already exists in the system, it will be **associated** with the operator;

ℹ️ **Already owned entities**

If the business entity was **created by the current operator**,
the claim is **already in place** and this endpoint **does not need to be called**.

⚠️ **Important – Enrollment vs registration**

This endpoint **must NOT be used** if the business entity has already been enrolled
through the **enrollment endpoint**.
Enrollment automatically handles all creation and association.

put.summary: Update an operator business entity management configuration
put.description:
🎯 **Purpose**

Update the **claim configuration** for a business entity **already owned by the current operator**.

⚠️ **Ownership requirement**

The business entity **must already belong to the current operator**.
This endpoint **cannot be used to claim or associate** a new entity.

⚠️ **Warning – Full replacement**

Claim configuration updates are **full replacements**.
You must always provide the **complete configuration object**;
any field not included in the update request will be **removed**.

parameters.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

delete.summary: Remove an operator business entity management
delete.description:
🎯 **Purpose**

Remove a business entity from the operator directory.

✅ **Eligibility condition**

A business entity **can be unclaimed only if there is no active registration associated with it**.
If at least one active registration exists, the unclaim operation will be **rejected**.

⚠️ **Important**

Once the unclaim operation succeeds, **all `operatorRelation` data will be permanently removed**.

parameters.name: customer-id
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

parameters.name: businessEntityId
parameters.description: The business entity id.

204.description: Business entity link with operator removed

401.description: Unauthorized - Authentication is required and has failed or has not yet been provided.

403.description: Forbidden - The server understood the request, but it refuses to authorize it.

404.description: Failed to remove link with operator

post.description:
🎯 **Purpose**

Execute the **registration of a business entity** in the **target network** using the provided identifier.

🌐 **Network behavior**

- **International network**:
  - Registers the entity in the **PEPPOL SML (SMK in PreProduction)**
  - Updates the **PEPPOL Directory**
- **French domestic network**:
  - Only **French business entities** can be registered
  - Performs the same PEPPOL registrations
  - Additionally updates the French **PPF directory**

🇫🇷 **French network scope**

Registration in the **French domestic network** is **restricted to French-to-French invoicing**.
To **send or receive invoices with international PEPPOL participants**,
the business entity **must also be registered in the international network**.

⚠️ **Important – Enrollment vs registration**

This endpoint **must NOT be used** if the business entity has already been enrolled
through the **enrollment endpoint**.
Enrollment automatically handles all required registrations.

🔑 **Identifier requirement**

- The **`businessEntityIdentifierId`** must be used to perform the registration.
- The **`businessEntityId` must NOT be used** for this operation.

⏱️ **Timeout requirement**

Implementers **must configure a timeout of at least 30 seconds** for this endpoint.

This operation performs calls to the **PEPPOL Directory API**.
The PEPPOL Directory service is **known to be intermittently unavailable or slow**,
which can legitimately lead to **long response times**.

Using a shorter timeout may result in **false failures** even though the registration
is still being processed successfully.

parameters.name: customer-id
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

parameters.name: businessEntityIdentifierId
parameters.description: The unique Iopole Id representing an identifier

parameters.name: targetDirectoryId
parameters.description: To be used only when multiple validity periods already exist

validityStartDate.description: {"text":"The date when the business entity will start to be considered as yours (only valid for French entities)","valueDescription":"YYYY-MM-DD","defaultValue":"If omitted, the default value is tomorrow","more":"Must be a future date"}

validityEndDate.description: {"text":"The date when the business entity will no longer be considered as yours (only valid for French entities)","valueDescription":"YYYY-MM-DD","defaultValue":"If omitted, no end date is set (ie. the registration remains valid indefinitely)","more":"Must be greater than validity start date"}

peppolMigrationKey.description: Migration Key to be used for migration from another SMP

allowRequestMigrationKey.description: If true, the system will try to retrieve a migration key from remote SMP

businessEntityNetworkRegisterWithPeppolM.summary: [Default][Peppol] With automatic peppol migration feature (only for Hermes)
[Default][Peppol] With peppol migration from migration key

post.description:
🎯 **Purpose**

Execute the **registration of a business entity** in the **target network** using the provided
**identifier scheme** and **identifier value**.

🌐 **Network behavior**

- **International network**:
  - Registers the entity in the **PEPPOL SML (SMK in PreProduction)**
  - Updates the **PEPPOL Directory**
- **French domestic network**:
  - Only **French business entities** can be registered
  - Performs the same PEPPOL registrations
  - Additionally updates the French **PPF directory**

🇫🇷 **French network scope**

Registration in the **French domestic network** is **restricted to French-to-French invoicing**.
To **send or receive invoices with international PEPPOL participants**,
the business entity **must also be registered in the international network**.

⚠️ **Important – Enrollment vs registration**

This endpoint **must NOT be used** if the business entity has already been enrolled
through the **enrollment endpoint**.
Enrollment automatically handles all required registrations.

🔑 **Identifier requirement**

- This operation must be called with the **identifier `scheme` and `value`**.

⏱️ **Timeout requirement**

Implementers **must configure a timeout of at least 30 seconds** for this endpoint.

This operation performs calls to the **PEPPOL Directory API**.
The PEPPOL Directory service is **known to be intermittently unavailable or slow**,
which can legitimately lead to **long response times**.

Using a shorter timeout may result in **false failures** even though the registration
is still being processed successfully.

delete.description:
🎯 **Purpose**

Remove a previously registered **identifier** from the target network using the provided
**directoryId** (available in the operator internal directory data).

🌐 **Network behavior**

- **International network**:
  - Removes the identifier from the **PEPPOL SML (SMK in PreProduction)**
  - Updates the **PEPPOL Directory**
- **French domestic network**:
  - Performs the same PEPPOL removal operations
  - Additionally removes the entry from the French **PPF directory**

⚠️ **Important notes**

- The `directoryId` must reference an **existing registration** owned by the operator.
- Removing a registration immediately makes the identifier **unreachable on the corresponding network**.
- This operation does **not delete the business entity**, only its **network registration**.

⏱️ **Timeout requirement**

Implementers **must configure a timeout of at least 30 seconds** for this endpoint.

This operation performs calls to the **PEPPOL Directory API**.
The PEPPOL Directory service is **known to be intermittently unavailable or slow**,
which can legitimately lead to **long response times**.

Using a shorter timeout may result in **false failures** even though the registration
is still being processed successfully.

put.description:
ℹ️ **Automatic 🇫🇷French entity Enrollment**

This endpoint **generates an enrollment link** for the end user.
Through this link, the end user completes the **enrollment process**, including
**KYC / KYB / mandate steps.**

The exact enrollment flow and required steps **depend on the operator configuration options**.

🚨 The enrollment process is only **fully automatic** BY DEFAULT.

The operator **does not need to create, claim, or register anything manually**.
Once the **end-user enrollment is completed**, the system automatically:
- creates all required resources,
- performs the necessary registrations,
- links them to the operator when applicable.

**No additional API call is required from the operator.**
**To change this default behavior, the operator can adjust the enrollment configuration options** to fit their specific needs and preferences.**Or contact the support**

**Registration Strategy**: You can override the operator-level registration configuration by providing the `registrationStrategy` field:
- `AUTO`: Registration is triggered immediately when enrollment is accepted (default)
- `MANUAL`: Registration must be triggered manually
- `NONE`: No automatic registration

siren.description: The SIREN number (9-digit identifier) of the business entity

registrationStrategy.description: Electronic address registration mode. AUTO: triggered immediately on acceptance, MANUAL: requires manual trigger, NONE: no registration. If not provided, uses operator-level configuration or defaults to AUTO

get.description:
Downloads a ZIP archive containing the enrollment evidence files for a completed enrollment.

The archive may include:
- **Identity verification report** (`kyc_evidence.pdf`)
- **Signed mandate** (`signed_mandate.pdf`) and **mandate audit trail** (`mandate_evidence.pdf`)

Each file is only included if the corresponding service was used during the enrollment.

🔒 This endpoint is restricted to **white label operators** only.

The enrollment must be in state `REGISTERING`, `REGISTERED`, or `COMPLETED`.

200.description: ZIP archive containing the enrollment evidence files

409.description: Conflict - The enrollment is not yet fully completed.

get.description:
📄 **Retrieve webhook configuration**

Returns the **details of a single webhook** identified by `webhookId`, as long as it is **linked to the authenticated user**.
The response includes the webhook configuration.

🔐 **Security & secrets**

Sensitive authentication values are **never returned in full clear text**.
When present, secret fields are returned in a **masked form**:
- the **first 5 characters** are shown
- the remaining characters are replaced with `****`

Example: `abcd1****`

ℹ️ **Notes**

- This endpoint is **read-only**.
- The returned payload represents the **current stored configuration** for each webhook.

put.description:
📝 **Update webhook**

This endpoint updates an existing webhook using a **mixed update strategy** depending on the data provided.

🔄 **Differential update (top-level fields)**

The following fields are updated **only if they are explicitly provided**:
- `active`

- `label`

- `filterStreamDirection`

- `adapterCode`


If any of these fields are omitted from the request, their existing values are **preserved**.

🔁 **Differential update (`interopData.endpoints`)**

> If `interopData.endpoints` is provided, each of the following endpoint configurations is processed independently:
> - `invoice`

> - `status`

> - `onboarding`

> - `events`

> - `authentication`

> 
> For each endpoint field:
> - **Omitted** → the existing configuration is **kept**
> - **Provided** → the configuration is **fully replaced**
> 
> ⚠️ **Important – no partial update inside an endpoint block**
> 
> Updates are **not partial** within a given endpoint configuration.
> If you provide an endpoint block (e.g., `authentication`), it must be **complete**: any sub-field not included in the request may be **lost/overwritten**.
> For example, providing `authentication` replaces the whole authentication configuration (callbacks and credentials) with the payload you send.

ex1.summary: Update only the registration status (differential top-level update)
ex1.description: Only updates the `status` field. All other top-level fields remain unchanged.

ex2.summary: Update only the label (differential top-level update)
ex2.description: Only updates the `label` field. All other top-level fields remain unchanged.

ex3.summary: Replace multiple interop endpoints at once (values provided are replaced)
ex3.description: Because `interopData.endpoints` is provided, each provided endpoint (`invoice`, `status`, `authentication`) is replaced with the given configuration. Omitted endpoint fields (e.g., `onboarding`) are kept as-is.

ex4.summary: Update only the invoice endpoint configuration (other endpoints kept)
ex4.description: Only replaces `interopData.endpoints.invoice`. Existing `status`, `onboarding`, and `authentication` endpoint configurations are preserved.

ex5.summary: Update only the authentication configuration (other endpoints kept)
ex5.description: Only replaces `interopData.endpoints.authentication`. Existing `invoice`, `status`, `onboarding`, and `events` endpoint configurations are preserved.

ex6.summary: Add/Update events endpoint (subscribe to specific events)
ex6.description: Configures the `events` endpoint to receive operator notifications. Only `INVOICE_INBOUND_INVALID` events will be delivered. Other endpoint configurations are preserved.

ex7.summary: Add/Update events endpoint (subscribe to all events)
ex7.description: Configures the `events` endpoint to receive ALL operator event types. Omitting `subscribedEvents` means all events are delivered. Other endpoint configurations are preserved.

ex8.description: Removes the `events` endpoint configuration by setting it to `null`. The operator will no longer receive event notifications. Other endpoint configurations are preserved.

delete.description:
🗑️ **Remove webhook (permanent deletion)**

This endpoint **permanently removes** a webhook identified by `webhookId`.
Once deleted, the webhook configuration and all associated data are **physically removed** from the system and **cannot be recovered**.

⚠️ **Important – irreversible operation**

Use this endpoint **only if you no longer need the webhook**.
If you want to **temporarily disable** a webhook while keeping it for future use, use the **PUT update endpoint** and set `active = false` instead.

ℹ️ **Notes**

- This operation is **destructive and final**.
- If the `webhookId` does not exist or is not linked to the authenticated user, a **404** is returned.

post.description:
🎯 **Purpose**

This endpoint is used by the **operator to configure a callback URL**.
The configured callback will later be **called by the platform** to notify the operator system
about asynchronous events (e.g., enrollment completion, inbound invoices, inbound/outbound status).

↔️ **Direction handling**

- In most cases, a **single bidirectional callback** is sufficient.
- The **direction** refers to the **invoice flow direction** (INBOUND / OUTBOUND). not the callback direction:
  - **INBOUND** → statuses related to **inbound invoices**
  - **OUTBOUND** → statuses related to **outbound invoices**

✅ **Callback requirements** (If not met, the notification may be retried as failed)

- The callback endpoint must accept **HTTP POST requests** for workflow execution.
- The callback endpoint **must return an HTTP 2xx** status code to acknowledge receipt.
- The callback endpoint **must NOT** return plain text / test text *(e.g., OK, received, test)*.
- The callback response **must be empty** *(no body)* **or** a **valid JSON** body.
- The callback endpoint **must respond within 60 seconds**.
- The callback endpoint should accept data **up to 100Mb**.

🔒 **Callback URL validation** (If checks fail, the callback configuration is rejected)

- Only **HTTPS** URLs are accepted.
- The hostname must be **DNS-resolvable**.
- The resolved IP address **must not be private or internal**.
- The endpoint must be **reachable via an HTTP GET request** (used for probe).
- **HTTP redirects are allowed** (up to a limited number).
- The endpoint must respond within a **short timeout**.

ℹ️ **Callback details**

Detailed information about the **callback payloads, headers, and delivery behavior**
is available in the **Callbacks section of this Swagger specification**.

🔐 **Security (production)**

In production environments, the configured callback endpoint **MUST be protected by an authentication mechanism**
(e.g., HMAC, OAuth2, ...).

All authentication secrets are **validated for strength and security**:
- Weak, common, or repetitive values are **rejected** (e.g., `password`, `test`, `changeme`).
- Secrets must meet **minimum length and entropy requirements**.
- **Machine-generated secrets** (hex or base64) are strongly recommended and fully supported.

🔁 **Retry strategy (production)**

The operator **should configure a retry strategy** using the **dedicated retry configuration endpoint**
to ensure reliable delivery in case of temporary failures or downstream unavailability.

parameters.name: customer-id
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

post.description:
Callback invoked by the platform to **deliver an inbound invoice** to the operator system.

> ✅ **Requirements**
> - Return HTTP **2xx** to acknowledge receipt
> - Response body must be **empty** or **valid JSON** (no plain text)
> - Must respond within **60 seconds**
> - Should accept data up to **100MB**

Callback invoked by the platform to **deliver invoice status updates** to the operator system.

> ✅ **Requirements**
> - Return HTTP **2xx** to acknowledge receipt
> - Response body must be **empty** or **valid JSON** (no plain text)
> - Must respond within **60 seconds**
> - Should accept data up to **100MB**

code.description: Status allowed to be set on an invoice.
Status allowed to be set on an invoice.

exampleStatusSubmittedResponse.description: The invoice has been received and is **currently being processed** by the platform (outbound invoices only).

exampleStatusIssuedResponse.description: The invoice is valid, routing succeeded, and it has been **sent to the buyer** (outbound invoices only).

exampleStatusReceivedResponse.description: The invoice has been **received and validated** by the **remote platform** (outbound invoices only).

exampleStatusMadeAvailableResponse.description: The invoice has been **received and made available to the buyer** (outbound invoices only).

exampleStatusRejectedResponse.summary: Status Rejected (✅ Always emitted when relevant)
exampleStatusRejectedResponse.description:
The invoice has been **rejected**.
Rejection details are available in the **status details**.

This status may be emitted by the **Iopole platform** or by the **remote platform** (outbound invoices only).

post.description:
Callback invoked by the platform to **notify the operator system** about the current **onboarding state**.

> ✅ **Requirements**
> - Return HTTP **2xx** to acknowledge receipt
> - Response body must be **empty** or **valid JSON** (no plain text)
> - Must respond within **60 seconds**

post.description:
Callback invoked by the platform to **notify the operator** about **generic events**.

> 🎯 **Event Types**
> - `INVOICE_INBOUND_INVALID` - Notifies when an invalid inbound invoice is received
>
> ✅ **Requirements**
> - Return HTTP **2xx** to acknowledge receipt
> - Response body must be **empty** or **valid JSON** (no plain text)
> - Must respond within **60 seconds**

post.description:
🎯 **Purpose**

Create a retry strategy used by the platform to **retry asynchronous deliveries**
(e.g., webhook notifications, invoice or status deliveries) when temporary failures occur.

Use this configuration to define the **number of attempts** and the **delay/backoff policy** between retries,
improving reliability when the operator system or downstream services are temporarily unavailable.

🔄 **PUSH mode only**

This endpoint is **not relevant in PULL mode**.
Retry strategies apply only to **PUSH-based deliveries** (e.g., webhooks).

🧪 **Recommendation**

In production environments, configuring a retry strategy is **strongly recommended**.

parameters.name: customer-id
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

requestBody.description: Create a retry strategy (available for all webhooks)

retryDelays.description: Explicit list of delays (in seconds) between retries

retryOn.description: List of HTTP status codes that should trigger a retry

retryOnEtimedout.description: Retry when the request times out (ETIMEDOUT).

retryOnEservfail.description: Retry when the DNS server returns a failure (ESERVFAIL).

retryOnEconnreset.description: Retry when the connection is unexpectedly closed by the peer (ECONNRESET).

retryOnEconnrefused.description: Retry when the connection attempt is refused by the remote host (ECONNREFUSED).

retryOnEnotfound.description: Retry when the target host or domain cannot be found (ENOTFOUND).

retryOnEaiAgain.description: Retry when a temporary DNS resolution error occurs (EAI_AGAIN).

retryOnEpipe.description: Retry when a broken pipe error occurs during a write operation (EPIPE).

retryOnEhostunreach.description: Retry when the target host is unreachable (EHOSTUNREACH).

retryOnEnetunreach.description: Retry when the network is unreachable (ENETUNREACH).

networkRetryOn.description:
Retry strategy based on low-level network errors.

 ⚠️ Activating some of these options can lead to **duplicate requests**, for example when a timeout occurs because the server takes too long to respond but eventually processes the request. These options should be enabled only if the target system is idempotent or protected against duplicate processing.

schema.description: The retry strategy

ex1.summary: Minimalist default Retry strategy (retry for 5XX errors and after 1, 3 and 6 minutes
